title: picea meyeri subsp. mongolica
intro:
Picea meyeri subsp. mongolica, also known as Mongolian silver fir, is a coniferous evergreen tree species native to northern China and Mongolia, characterized by its slender trunk and silvery-blue foliage.

This tree has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine, and its medicinal properties include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe respiratory issues and reduce fever.

The health benefits of Picea meyeri subsp. mongolica include its ability to help alleviate congestion, coughs, and sore throats, while also providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help to protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being.

In herbal preparations, the needles and bark of Picea meyeri subsp. mongolica are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, fever, and skin infections.
